page: Microsoft Azure E4as_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the EASv5 family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. The server features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, but it is Premium Storage capable. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, and average in Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics show limitations. Multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench is weak, placing in the bottom 25%, which is consistent with its 2 physical cores. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks fall into the bottom 25%, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is poor, ranking in the bottom 10%. Database operations and Redis SET operations also register in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4as_v5 offers a high memory density relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require intensive multi-core processing or high memory bandwidth. It is suitable for relational databases with moderate transaction volumes, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure E4as_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EASv5 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable. While single-core CPU performance is strong, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the bottom 25%. This server is suited for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute demands, such as caching layers and database operations.
